companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# Kima Hub Upstream — Import Optimization

## Document Status
- Last updated: 2026-03-02
- Feature: Import optimization adoption from Kima Hub v1.6.0 (KH-22, KH-23, KH-24)
- Scope: Implementation
- Planning maturity: `Spec Outline` complete, `Refined Spec` complete, `Detailed Implementation Plan` complete
- Implementation state: Backlog candidate (not started)

## Origin

Upstream: `chevron7locked/kima-hub` v1.5.0 → v1.6.0 changelog analysis.

## Goal

Fix import pagination truncation, optimize playlist building query performance, and add defensive batching to Lidarr album fetching.

## Spec Outline

### KH-22: Import Pagination Beyond API Limits
- **Priority**: Medium-Low | **Effort**: 1-2 hours
- Spotify silently truncates at 100 tracks, Deezer at 25 tracks.
- Verify cursor-based pagination loops exist; fix if truncation found.
- Add partial-result error recovery on rate limiting.
- Files: `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`, Deezer import service

### KH-23: Import Batch Query Optimization
- **Priority**: Medium-Low | **Effort**: 2-3 hours
- `buildPlaylist()` may execute ~3000 individual per-track DB queries.
- Optimize to 2 batch queries: batch-load all library tracks, match imported tracks in memory.
- `MATCH_BATCH_SIZE = 25` and `MATCH_BATCH_CONCURRENCY = 2` exist — verify if `matchTrack()` does individual DB queries.
- Files: `backend/src/services/playlistImportService.ts`, `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`

### KH-24: Lidarr Batched Album Fetching
- **Priority**: Medium-Low | **Effort**: 1 hour
- V8 string overflow when `JSON.parse()` receives >512MB response from a single Lidarr API call.
- Fetch per-artist in batches of 25 to prevent overflow.
- Defensive improvement even if not currently hitting the limit.
- Files: `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`

## Refined Spec

### Constraints
1. No import API contract changes.
2. No database schema changes.
3. Import behavior identical — same tracks imported, same matching logic.
4. No new import sources.

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Imports correctly paginate beyond default API page sizes.
2. Playlist building uses batch queries instead of N+1 per-track lookups.
3. Lidarr album fetching handles large libraries without V8 crash.

## Detailed Implementation Plan (Atomic Tasks)

### Task 1: KH-22 — Import Pagination Audit
- Status: Pending
- Deliverable: Verified pagination beyond API limits with rate limiting recovery

### Task 2: KH-23 — Batch Query Optimization
- Status: Pending
- Deliverable: Batch-loaded library tracks with in-memory matching

### Task 3: KH-24 — Lidarr Batched Fetching
- Status: Pending
- Deliverable: Per-artist album fetching in groups of 25

plan.title: Kima Hub Upstream — Import Optimization
plan.objective: Adopt three import/integration optimization items from Kima Hub v1.6.0 (KH-22, KH-23, KH-24): audit pagination beyond API limits, batch query optimization for playlist building, and Lidarr batched album fetching to prevent V8 string overflow.
in_scope: Audit and fix import pagination beyond API-imposed page limits
Imports correctly paginate beyond Spotify 100-track and Deezer 25-track limits
Optimize import batch queries — replace N+1 per-track DB queries with batch loading
Playlist building uses 2 batch queries with in-memory matching instead of ~3000 individual queries
Implement batched album fetching for Lidarr integration
Lidarr album API calls batched per-artist in groups of 25 to prevent V8 string overflow
out_of_scope: No new import sources. No import UI changes. No migration of import service architecture. No changes to Spotify anonymous URL parsing.

tasks.summary: KH-22: Audit import pagination for Spotify and Deezer; fix any silent truncation at API page limits
acceptance_criteria: Pagination verified beyond default page sizes
Rate limiting recovery implemented
Test coverage for multi-page scenarios

tasks.summary: KH-23: Replace N+1 per-track DB queries in playlist building with batch loading and in-memory matching
acceptance_criteria: Batch query replaces individual per-track lookups
Match results identical to current behavior
Performance improved on large imports

tasks.summary: KH-24: Implement per-artist batched album fetching for Lidarr to prevent V8 string overflow
acceptance_criteria: Albums fetched in batches of 25 per artist
No crash on large Lidarr libraries
Behavior identical to current single-call approach

tasks.summary: Run verification for resumed soundspan work
acceptance_criteria: Run the repo-defined ACM verification tests before completion.

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals: Adopt three import/integration optimization items from Kima Hub v1.6.0 (KH-22, KH-23, KH-24): audit pagination beyond API limits, batch query optimization for playlist building, and Lidarr batched album fetching to prevent V8 string overflow.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals: No new import sources. No import UI changes. No migration of import service architecture. No changes to Spotify anonymous URL parsing.

spec_outline.summary: Three import pipeline optimizations: verify cursor-based pagination beyond API page limits (Spotify 100, Deezer 25), replace N+1 per-track DB queries with batch loading for playlist building, and implement per-artist batching for Lidarr album fetching to prevent V8 string overflow on large libraries.

full_spec_outline.objective: Audit and fix import pagination beyond API-imposed page limits
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Imports correctly paginate beyond Spotify 100-track and Deezer 25-track limits
acceptance_criteria: Spotify import uses cursor-based pagination beyond 100-track limit
Deezer import paginates beyond 25-track default
Partial-result error recovery on rate limiting
Test coverage for multi-page imports

full_spec_outline.objective: Optimize import batch queries — replace N+1 per-track DB queries with batch loading
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Playlist building uses 2 batch queries with in-memory matching instead of ~3000 individual queries
acceptance_criteria: matchTrack() uses batch-loaded library tracks instead of per-track DB queries
Performance improvement measurable on large imports (>500 tracks)
No behavior change in match results

full_spec_outline.objective: Implement batched album fetching for Lidarr integration
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Lidarr album API calls batched per-artist in groups of 25 to prevent V8 string overflow
acceptance_criteria: Album fetching uses per-artist batching in groups of 25
No V8 string overflow on large Lidarr libraries (>10,000 albums)
Defensive improvement even if not currently hitting the limit

refined_spec.summary: All three items are backend-only. KH-22 is an audit task — verify existing pagination, fix if truncation found. KH-23 is a query pattern optimization in the import service. KH-24 is a defensive batching improvement for Lidarr API calls. No schema changes.

full_refined_spec.decision: Optimize existing import paths without changing import service architecture or adding new import sources
full_refined_spec.rationale: These are performance and reliability improvements to existing working flows. The V8 string overflow in KH-24 is a latent crash that only manifests with large Lidarr libraries.
acceptance_criteria: No import API contract changes
No database schema changes
Import behavior is identical (same tracks imported)
Performance measurably improved for KH-23

implementation_plan.summary: Three independent steps, one per KH item. Can be done in parallel. KH-22 is primarily an audit. KH-23 is the highest-impact optimization. KH-24 is the most defensive.

steps.deliverable: KH-22: Audit import pagination for Spotify and Deezer; fix any silent truncation at API page limits
acceptance_criteria: Pagination verified beyond default page sizes
Rate limiting recovery implemented
Test coverage for multi-page scenarios

steps.deliverable: KH-23: Replace N+1 per-track DB queries in playlist building with batch loading and in-memory matching
acceptance_criteria: Batch query replaces individual per-track lookups
Match results identical to current behavior
Performance improved on large imports

steps.deliverable: KH-24: Implement per-artist batched album fetching for Lidarr to prevent V8 string overflow
acceptance_criteria: Albums fetched in batches of 25 per artist
No crash on large Lidarr libraries
Behavior identical to current single-call approach
